title: Chronos
subtitle: The timeline. 1981 → 2026. One station per year, more when the year had more.

items.label: Born
items.detail: Phyathai Hospital, Bangkok. A Sunday.

stations.photo_caption: Siam Square. The columns. Age six.

items.label: First memory
items.detail: Pantip Plaza, near Siam Paragon, age six. Classical columns in the interior — Doric, possibly Ionic. Still unverified.

stations.photo_caption: The rebuilt house. Grandmother's land, after the expressway.

items.label: The house
items.detail: The current house was built. Grandmother's land had been taken by eminent domain for expressway construction. The family rebuilt. Forty-two years and counting at the same address.

items.label: Undergraduate, B.Arch
items.detail: Architecture and Urban Design. The instrument was first picked up here: read the space as evidence.

items.label: M.Sc, MIT
items.detail: Cambridge, Massachusetts. The architecture that built MIT was the lesson, not the curriculum.

items.label: M.Phil, Oxford
items.detail: Two years. The blog's later affection for Hume began here.

items.label: Begin PhD, Harvard
items.detail: Anthropology. The pivot from architect to anthropologist was already complete in private; the institutional move was the slow part.

stations.photo_caption: Grandmother. The land before the loss.

items.label: Shanghai, Yangpu District
items.detail: Two years of fieldwork. Huangpu River on one side, Fudan on the other. Fuzhou Road for the books.

items.label: First coffee-shop essay
items.detail: Drafted on the Yangtze. The line about access to modernity was written there.

items.label: Dissertation defended
items.detail: Harvard, Anthropology. The institutional move complete.

items.label: Blog begins
items.detail: nonharvard.wordpress.com. First entries on writing, modernity, and the Bangkok of the late 1990s.

items.label: Tradition: Absolutely nothing.
items.detail: The shortest sentence in the corpus. Day 5 of the blog. The voice is now fully its own.

items.label: Pandemic year
items.detail: Brompton mileage triples. Coffee-shop essays multiply. The Leibniz-and-bike argument is made fully on Day 3.

items.label: SCITI begins
items.detail: Smart City Thailand Index. The methodology that would later catch most cities branding ahead of performing.

items.label: SCITI v2 launches
items.detail: Smart City Summit, Taipei. 174 cities, five pillars, AMPI scoring.

items.label: Day 70, Harvard definition
items.detail: Harvard: A for-profit company in Massachusetts authorized by law to act as a single entity that thinks of itself as an educational institution. This sentence is now a teaching tool.

items.label: Blog Day 1
items.detail: What Does Writing Do to Me. 1,888 words. The instrument announced.

items.label: Blog Day 100
items.detail: The 100-day writing project concludes. The corpus stands at ~240,000 words.

items.label: GITEX AI Asia, Singapore
items.detail: Speaking on Smart City Thailand. The portrait that lives on the SUBJECT page was taken here.

items.label: 100 Days of Non begins
items.detail: Day 001. Memory and record, side by side, for 100 days. The disagreement is the most interesting cell on the page.

items.label: The museum opens
items.detail: Eleven rooms. One bot, soon. A journal that is also a journey.
